questions.question: What is matter?
questions.answer: Anything that has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: What is meant by particulate nature of matter?
questions.answer: Matter is made up of extremely small particles.

questions.question: Name the basic building blocks of matter.
Do particles of matter have spaces between them?
What force holds particles of matter together?
Name the three states of matter.
Which state of matter has a definite shape and volume?
Which state of matter takes the shape of its container?
Which state of matter has neither definite shape nor volume?
What property allows gases to be compressed easily?
What is diffusion?
questions.answer: The spreading of particles from one place to another.

questions.question: Give one example of diffusion.
questions.answer: Perfume smell spreading in a room.

questions.question: Which state of matter shows the fastest diffusion?
What is melting?
questions.answer: The process of changing a solid into a liquid.

questions.question: What is boiling?
questions.answer: The process of changing a liquid into a gas at its boiling point.

questions.question: What is evaporation?
questions.answer: The slow change of liquid into vapor from its surface.

questions.question: At what temperature does ice melt?
What happens to particle motion when temperature increases?
Which state of matter has the strongest interparticle attraction?
Which state of matter has the weakest interparticle attraction?
What is the boiling point of water?
Which process converts liquid into gas below boiling point?
What are fluids?
questions.answer: Liquids and gases that can flow.

questions.question: Which experiment shows compressibility of gases?
What is the main idea of particle theory?
questions.answer: Matter is made of tiny particles that are in constant motion.
